howItWorks.intro: This crypto profit/loss calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: P/L
rules.explanation: Simple profit calculation with fee deduction.

howItWorks.outro: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

faq.question: How is crypto profit calculated?
faq.answer: (Sell Price - Buy Price) × Quantity - Fees = Net Profit.

faq.question: Do I owe taxes on crypto gains?
faq.answer: Yes, crypto gains are taxed as capital gains. Short-term gains are taxed as income.

faq.question: Is the Crypto Profit/Loss Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard cryptocurrency and blockchain formulas trusted by crypto investors and traders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What buy price should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate buy price value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Crypto Profit/Loss Calculator?
sections.body: The Crypto Profit/Loss Calculator is a free online tool designed for crypto investors and traders who need quick, accurate calculations in the cryptocurrency and blockchain space. By entering your buy price, sell price, quantity, you get instant results including profit/loss, roi, net after fees. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting profit/loss right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In cryptocurrency and blockchain, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crypto investors and traders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to evaluate your crypto portfolio with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your buy price and need to find the right profit/loss. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Buy Price ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your buy price — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: sell price, quantity, total fees. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your profit/loss, roi, net after fees. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your buy price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both profit/loss and roi — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to evaluate your crypto portfolio.
If you're unsure about your total fees, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
